# GitHub repo setup (SEO for "polymarket trading bot")

Set these on your repo page: click the **gear icon** next to "About".

---

## 1. Description (About)

**Do not** repeat "polymarket trading bot" many times. GitHub ranks better with a **short, readable** description (under 120 characters) that includes the keyword naturally.

**Paste this:**

```
Polymarket trading bot — copy trading bot that mirrors trades in real time with auto-redemption.
```

(≈ 72 characters.)

---

## 2. Topics

Add these (exact spelling). More topics = more ways to match search and appear on topic pages.

| Topic |
|-------|
| `polymarket` |
| `polymarket-trading-bot` |
| `polymarket-copy-trading-bot` |
| `polymarket-bot` |
| `copy-trading` |
| `trading-bot` |
| `prediction-markets` |
| `mirror-trading` |
| `polygon` |
| `clob` |

---

## 3. License (SEO & discoverability)

**Why it matters:** Users can filter GitHub search by license (e.g. `polymarket trading bot license:mit` or `license:isc`). Repos **without** a detected license are excluded from those results. License also appears in search results and the repo sidebar, which can affect trust and clicks.

**What we have:** This repo has a **LICENSE** file (ISC) in the root and `"license": "ISC"` in `package.json`, so GitHub will detect it and show it. No extra step needed on the website—just keep the LICENSE file in the repo.

**Optional:** MIT is the most searched license (`license:mit`). If you want to maximize visibility for license-filtered searches, you could switch to MIT; ISC is equivalent for most use cases and is already set.

---

## Why the other repo can rank higher

- **More topics** (10 vs 3) → more relevance signals.
- **Keyword stuffing** in About can hurt; short, natural text ranks better.
- **Forks** may be used as an engagement signal.

Your **repo name** and **stars** are strong; fixing About + topics + having a license helps.
